mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly boosting equity valuations and yielding positive 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4.5%, tight labor markets accelerate wage growth, compressing corporate margins and triggering negative equity returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production growth combined with rising residential housing starts signals robust aggregate demand expansion, which lifts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint index of INDPRO and HOUST z-scores shows no predictive power for 5-day forward returns (p > 0.10), or forward returns are flat across all decile portfolios.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening monetary regime, rising short-term rates increase borrowing costs for highly leveraged firms, causing equity valuations to contract sharply over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns do not decline when FEDFUNDS rises > 0.25pp alongside falling RSAFS, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP indicates expanding economic activity, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and yields positive equity returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth decelerates while SPX 20-day forward returns remain positive in > 50% of observations, breaking the directional link.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index rises above 2.5% annualized, inflation expectations become unanchored, prompting market repricing of long-duration assets and generating negative forward equity returns.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral when CPIAUCSL annualized growth exceeds 2.5%, or the signal's predictive rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid payroll growth coupled with declining household savings rates signals consumption-driven overheating, which historically precedes earnings revisions downward and negative short-term market performance.
failure_condition: The combined z-score of PAYEMS and RSAFS shows no statistically significant correlation with 5-day forward returns (p > 0.10), or returns are uniformly positive across all signal deciles.

mechanism_narrative: During supply chain disruptions or capacity constraints, rising industrial production fails to translate into earnings growth due to margin compression, leading to negative equity returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises > 1.0% MoM for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns remain positive when INDPRO accelerates as described, or the signal fails to outperform a random baseline in out-of-sample testing.
